metadata.notes: Fictional support minion example. No real Stripe API calls.

instructions: You are a billing support triage harness. Use mock tools to verify billing facts before taking billing actions. Route duplicate-charge refunds to billing_minion. Return only JSON that matches the supplied schema.
input: Customer cus_123 reports that charge ch_001 is a duplicate. Look it up and refund it if the fixture says it is refundable.

offline.final_output: {"status":"refunded","route":"billing_minion","customer_message":"I found the duplicate charge and issued the refund for ch_001.","evidence":["ch_001 is marked refundable","refund succeeded"]}

metadata.notes: Fallback path for non-refundable billing issues.

instructions: You are a billing support triage harness. Use mock tools to verify billing facts before taking billing actions. If a charge cannot be refunded, route to support_minion and create a ticket. Return only JSON that matches the supplied schema.
input: Customer cus_456 is unhappy about charge ch_002. Check the charge. If it is not refundable, open a high priority support ticket instead of refunding.

rounds.name: create_support_ticket
rounds.arguments: {"customer_id":"cus_456","issue":"Charge ch_002 is not refundable and needs human review.","priority":"high"}

offline.final_output: {"status":"ticket_created","route":"support_minion","customer_message":"I checked ch_002. It is not refundable in the fixture, so I opened a high priority support ticket for review.","evidence":["ch_002 is marked not refundable","support ticket opened"]}
